key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

annotations.action_guidance: lie on your back with your legs naturally bent about 90 degrees, keep your legs hip_x0002_width apart, and relax your feet. place your hands naturally on both sides of your body with palms down, and keep your spine in a standard neutral position. when inhaling, keep your body stable, and when exhaling, contract the abdominal muscles, pull your navel toward the spine, tilt the pelvis backward and lift your pubis. continue to exhale, raise your spine from the tailbone section by section to roll off the mat until your knees and hips are raised in a straight line with the shoulders. inhale again, and keep your torso still. exhale, relax your chest and ribs, and turn the spine to the starting position, section by section.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.
annotations.comment: The movements are performed according to the instructional text.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.

Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

key_point_verification: Ensure your body forms a straight line when viewed from the side.
Place your hands on the mat on both sides of your chest.
Keep your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend your arms to lower your body.
Descend until your elbows are slightly above your torso.
Stretch your arms to lift your body back up.
Return to the initial kneeling position.
annotations.comment: Feet not crossed.
annotations.action_guidance: cross your feet, kneel on the mat, keep your back straight, keep your body in a straight line from the side, and put your hands on both sides of the chest, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. bend your arms and bend down until your elbows are slightly above your torso, then stretch your arms and get up to restore.

key_point_verification: Ensure your body forms a straight line when viewed from the side.

Stretch your arms to lift your body back up.

annotations.comment: The basic movements meet the requirements stated in the instructional text, except for one detail - the crossover of the feet did not meet the requirement.
annotations.action_guidance: cross your feet, kneel on the mat, keep your back straight, keep your body in a straight line from the side, and put your hands on both sides of the chest, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. bend your arms and bend down until your elbows are slightly above your torso, then stretch your arms and get up to restore.

key_point_verification: Lift your head and chest upward.
Tense your abdominal muscles for stability.
Use the strength of your pectoral (chest) muscles.
Clap your hands while performing the exercise.
Perform jumping movements back and forth.
annotations.comment: Non-alternating jumping with both legs forward and backward.
annotations.action_guidance: lift your head and chest, and tense your abdomen. tense the arms, and use the strength of your pectoral muscles to clap your hands while jumping back and forth with alternating feet.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.
annotations.comment: The hands are not positioned on both sides of the body.
annotations.action_guidance: lie on your back with your legs naturally bent about 90 degrees, keep your legs hip_x0002_width apart, and relax your feet. place your hands naturally on both sides of your body with palms down, and keep your spine in a standard neutral position. when inhaling, keep your body stable, and when exhaling, contract the abdominal muscles, pull your navel toward the spine, tilt the pelvis backward and lift your pubis. continue to exhale, raise your spine from the tailbone section by section to roll off the mat until your knees and hips are raised in a straight line with the shoulders. inhale again, and keep your torso still. exhale, relax your chest and ribs, and turn the spine to the starting position, section by section.

key_point_verification: Ensure your upper body is in a straight line with your back leg.
Place your hands on your hips.
From the lunge position, rise to a standing position.
As you get up, simultaneously raise the knee of your back leg.
Focus on moving steadily and smoothly as you transition from the lunge to the knee raise.
Avoid tilting your pelvis to the side during the lunge.
Keep your pelvis and upper body facing forward at all times.
annotations.comment: Did not maintain good balance.
annotations.action_guidance: in the lunge position, the upper body is in a straight line with the back leg, and put your hands on your hips. get up, raise your knees simultaneously, and move as steadily and smoothly as possible. do not tilt your pelvis to the side during the lunge, and keep your pelvis and upper body always forward.

key_point_verification: Keep your chest lifted and abdominal muscles engaged.
Place both hands on your hips.
Step back with your right leg.
Squat down, ensuring that the knee joint of the front leg and the ankle joint are aligned vertically.
Distribute your weight evenly across both legs.
Bend both knees, lowering your body until the knee of your right leg is close to the ground.
Push back up to return to the initial standing position.
annotations.comment: Not yet fully returned to an upright posture.
annotations.action_guidance: keep your chest up and your abdominal muscles tight, and look ahead with both hands on your hips. step back with your right leg, squat down so that the knee joint and ankle joint are in the same vertical line, and keep the load evenly distributed on both legs; sink your hip, bend the knees and squat down until your right leg knee joint is close to the ground, and then return.

key_point_verification: Lift your legs alternately and quickly.
Keep the balls of your feet (front feet) in contact with the ground during the lifts.
Maintain a stable upper body throughout the exercise.
Swing your arms in rhythm with the leg lifts.
Aim to maintain the fastest speed possible while performing the leg lifts.
annotations.comment: The hands are not raised.
annotations.action_guidance: straighten your back, look ahead, and lift your legs alternately and quickly with your front feet on the ground. keep your body stable and swing your arms with the rhythm of the leg lifts. maintain the fastest speed.

annotations.action_guidance: straighten your back, look ahead, and lift your legs alternately and quickly with your front feet on the ground. keep your body stable and swing your arms with the rhythm of the leg lifts. maintain the fastest speed.

key_point_verification: Lie on the mat with your back flat.
Extend your arms straight above your head.
Engage your abdominal muscles to initiate rising up.
Lift your head, shoulders, upper back, and then lower back off the ground in turn.
Reach forward to touch your feet with your hands.
Ensure your lower back, upper back, shoulders, and head touch the ground sequentially as you lower back down.
Maintain control over your movement throughout the exercise. Aim for a frequency of about one repetition every 2 seconds.
annotations.comment: Failure to touch the feet with both hands, with exertion on the hands and neck, and not fully utilizing abdominal strength.
annotations.action_guidance: lie on the mat, put your arms straight on either side of your head, put your feet together, open your legs, and use your abdominal muscles to get up. keep your head, shoulders, upper back, and lower back off the ground in turn, and touch your feet with your hands. make sure your lower back, upper back, shoulder, and head touch the ground in turn when lowering. control your speed throughout the action, with the frequency of about once every 2 seconds.

key_point_verification: Stand with your feet about twice shoulder-width apart.
Position your toes so they are pointing diagonally forward.
Squat down until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Ensure your knees are aligned in the same direction as your toes.
Keep your upper body as straight as possible throughout the movement.
Slightly sit back as you squat down, like sitting into a chair.
Cross your arms over your chest.
annotations.comment: Both hands are not crossed in front of the chest.
annotations.action_guidance: stand about twice shoulder-width apart, with your toes facing diagonally forward. when squatting to the thighs parallel to the ground, keep your knees in the same direction as your toes. keep your upper body as straight as possible, and sit back slightly when squatting. cross your arms over your chest.

Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

Lift your arms using shoulder strength.
Press your arms down using the strength of your back.
Use the movement of your arms to help drive your body to jump.
Perform the jump by opening and closing your feet.
Relax your calves as much as possible during the jump.
Maintain a steady head position, avoiding lowering or raising your head.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.
annotations.comment: The movements are completed in accordance with the instructional text, with a normal speed and high fluidity.
annotations.action_guidance: lie on your back with your legs naturally bent about 90 degrees, keep your legs hip_x0002_width apart, and relax your feet. place your hands naturally on both sides of your body with palms down, and keep your spine in a standard neutral position. when inhaling, keep your body stable, and when exhaling, contract the abdominal muscles, pull your navel toward the spine, tilt the pelvis backward and lift your pubis. continue to exhale, raise your spine from the tailbone section by section to roll off the mat until your knees and hips are raised in a straight line with the shoulders. inhale again, and keep your torso still. exhale, relax your chest and ribs, and turn the spine to the starting position, section by section.

Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.
annotations.comment: The movements are basically in line with the instructional text, but the distance between the legs is slightly too wide and the upward movement of the abdomen is a bit insufficient.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.
annotations.comment: The movements followed the instructional text, the speed was moderate, the execution was smooth, keep it up
annotations.action_guidance: lie on your back with your legs naturally bent about 90 degrees, keep your legs hip_x0002_width apart, and relax your feet. place your hands naturally on both sides of your body with palms down, and keep your spine in a standard neutral position. when inhaling, keep your body stable, and when exhaling, contract the abdominal muscles, pull your navel toward the spine, tilt the pelvis backward and lift your pubis. continue to exhale, raise your spine from the tailbone section by section to roll off the mat until your knees and hips are raised in a straight line with the shoulders. inhale again, and keep your torso still. exhale, relax your chest and ribs, and turn the spine to the starting position, section by section.

Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.
annotations.comment: This does not seem like a hip bridge, he is lifting his legs, but there is no requirement to do so.
annotations.action_guidance: lie on your back with your legs naturally bent about 90 degrees, keep your legs hip_x0002_width apart, and relax your feet. place your hands naturally on both sides of your body with palms down, and keep your spine in a standard neutral position. when inhaling, keep your body stable, and when exhaling, contract the abdominal muscles, pull your navel toward the spine, tilt the pelvis backward and lift your pubis. continue to exhale, raise your spine from the tailbone section by section to roll off the mat until your knees and hips are raised in a straight line with the shoulders. inhale again, and keep your torso still. exhale, relax your chest and ribs, and turn the spine to the starting position, section by section.

key_point_verification: Stand with your feet about twice shoulder-width apart.
Position your toes so they are pointing diagonally forward.
Squat down until your thighs are parallel to the ground.

Keep your upper body as straight as possible throughout the movement.
Slightly sit back as you squat down, like sitting into a chair.
Cross your arms over your chest.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.

Ensure your body forms a straight line when viewed from the side.
Place your hands on the mat on both sides of your chest.
Keep your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend your arms to lower your body towards the mat.
Descend until your elbows are slightly higher than your torso.
Stretch your arms to push your body back up.
Return to the initial push-up position.
annotations.comment: Sink lower.
annotations.action_guidance: push up on the mat, keep your waist and back straight, keep your body in a straight line from the side, and hold the hands on both sides of the chest, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. bend your arms and bend down your body until your elbows are slightly higher than your torso, then stretch your arms to get up and restore.

annotations.action_guidance: push up on the mat, keep your waist and back straight, keep your body in a straight line from the side, and hold the hands on both sides of the chest, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. bend your arms and bend down your body until your elbows are slightly higher than your torso, then stretch your arms to get up and restore.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.
annotations.comment: Not bad, keep practicing.
annotations.action_guidance: lie on your back with your legs naturally bent about 90 degrees, keep your legs hip_x0002_width apart, and relax your feet. place your hands naturally on both sides of your body with palms down, and keep your spine in a standard neutral position. when inhaling, keep your body stable, and when exhaling, contract the abdominal muscles, pull your navel toward the spine, tilt the pelvis backward and lift your pubis. continue to exhale, raise your spine from the tailbone section by section to roll off the mat until your knees and hips are raised in a straight line with the shoulders. inhale again, and keep your torso still. exhale, relax your chest and ribs, and turn the spine to the starting position, section by section.

Avoid tilting your pelvis to the side during the lunge.
Keep your pelvis and upper body facing forward at all times.

Distribute your weight evenly across both legs.
Bend both knees, lowering your body until the knee of your right leg is close to the ground.
Push back up to return to the initial standing position.

key_point_verification: Lie on your back with legs bent at about a 90-degree angle.
Keep legs hip-width apart and feet relaxed.
Place hands naturally by your sides with palms facing down.
Inhale and keep your body stable.
As you exhale, contract your abdominal muscles.
Pull your navel toward your spine, tilt your pelvis backward, and lift your pubis.
Continue exhaling and lift your spine off the mat, starting from the tailbone.
Progressively raise your body until your knees, hips, and shoulders align in a straight line.
Inhale again while keeping your torso lifted and still.
Exhale, relax your chest and ribs.
Gently lower your spine back to the mat, segment by segment, returning to the starting position.
